title: Today's Markets Brief: US gives Hungary’s MOL more time for talks to buy Serbian oil firm NIS
summary: A brief look at the day's market news, covering US talks with Hungary's MOL, Dow Jones Futures, and more.

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to your markets brief. Let's dive into today's top stories. First up, the US has given Hungary's MOL more time to negotiate the purchase of Serbian oil firm NIS. This move seems to be an extension of the deadline for talks, which could imply that there's some complexity in getting this deal done. MOL is looking to expand its presence in the region, and NIS is a key player in Serbia's oil and gas sector. The extra time might just be what MOL needs to iron out the details.

I'm curious to see how this deal plays out, as it could have implications for the regional energy landscape. It's also a reminder that even in a global market, local players can have a significant impact. Shifting gears, let's talk about Dow Jones Futures. The market rally is facing its first real test, and investors are watching closely. With a SpaceX IPO on the horizon and major players like Apple and Oracle looming, it's a lot to take in.

The market's been on a bit of a tear lately, so it's natural to wonder if this momentum can continue. What stands out to me is the sheer number of big names set to make some noise – it's like a heavyweight boxing match, but with earnings reports and economic data instead of punches. Oracle's earnings report, for instance, could provide some insight into the state of the tech sector.

My quick read: today's market story has that familiar split-screen feeling. There is real excitement around artificial intelligence and chips, but the room still keeps one eye on risk, because markets enjoy a celebration more when they know where the exits are.
